title: cacalia parasitica
intro: Cacalia parasitica, also known as Yellow Saxifrage, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal preparations, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, providing health benefits such as relieving symptoms of diarrhea, dysentery, and ulcers. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cacalia parasitica is often used in tea or tincture form, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
